heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Akira Kurosawa. Written by Akira Kurosawa, Hideo Oguni, and Masato Ide.
clean: Directed by Akira Kurosawa. Written by Akira Kurosawa, Hideo Oguni, and Masato Ide.

heading: Kyoami
raw: Man is born crying. When he has cried enough, he dies.
clean: Man is born crying. When he has cried enough, he dies.

heading: Kyoami
raw: Why stay with this mad old man? If the rock you stay on starts to roll, jump clean. Or you'll go with it and be squashed. Only a fool stays aboard.
clean: Why stay with this mad old man? If the rock you stay on starts to roll, jump clean. Or you'll go with it and be squashed. Only a fool stays aboard.

heading: Kyoami
raw: The failed mind sees the heart's failings.
clean: The failed mind sees the heart's failings.

heading: Kyoami
raw: In a mad world, only the mad are sane!
clean: In a mad world, only the mad are sane!

heading: Kyoami
raw: He gives away his house, He gives away his lands, He now holds a new title: 'Lord of the rice fields' a scarecrow!
clean: He gives away his house, He gives away his lands, He now holds a new title: 'Lord of the rice fields' a scarecrow!

heading: Saburo
raw: Does a traitor betray himself?
clean: Does a traitor betray himself?

heading: Tango
raw: Men prefer sorrow over joy... suffering over peace!
clean: Men prefer sorrow over joy... suffering over peace!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: The Great Lord goes nowhere alone.
clean: The Great Lord goes nowhere alone.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jiro: You renounced your power. You have no need of an escort.
clean: You renounced your power. You have no need of an escort.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: Only the birds and the beasts live in solitude.
clean: Only the birds and the beasts live in solitude.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kurogane: Saburo is not our only enemy.
clean: Saburo is not our only enemy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jiro: So what? If they attack, we retaliate. We grab their land and enlarge our own.
clean: So what? If they attack, we retaliate. We grab their land and enlarge our own.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kurogane: Fine words, but words don't win wars.
clean: Fine words, but words don't win wars.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: I am lost...
clean: I am lost...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kyoami: Such is the human condition.
clean: Such is the human condition.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kyoami: A serpent's egg is white and pure. A bird's is speckled and soiled.
clean: A serpent's egg is white and pure. A bird's is speckled and soiled.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: This is a castle... Here's a wall.
clean: This is a castle... Here's a wall.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kyoami: The bird left the speckled egg for the white.
clean: The bird left the speckled egg for the white.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: Strange...
clean: Strange...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kyoami: The egg cracks; out comes a snake.
clean: The egg cracks; out comes a snake.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: Empty space above the wall. Why?
clean: Empty space above the wall. Why?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kyoami: The bird is gobbled by the snake.
clean: The bird is gobbled by the snake.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: Where am I? Who am I?
clean: Where am I? Who am I?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kyoami: Stupid bird!
clean: Stupid bird!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Kyoami: Are there no gods... no Buddha? If you exist, hear me. You are mischievous and cruel! Are you so bored up there you must crush us like ants? Is it such fun to see men weep?
clean: Are there no gods... no Buddha? If you exist, hear me. You are mischievous and cruel! Are you so bored up there you must crush us like ants? Is it such fun to see men weep?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tango: Enough! Do not blaspheme! It is the gods who weep. They see us killing each other over and over since time began. They can't save us from ourselves.
clean: Enough! Do not blaspheme! It is the gods who weep. They see us killing each other over and over since time began. They can't save us from ourselves.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: What madness have I spoken? Wherein lies my senility?
clean: What madness have I spoken? Wherein lies my senility?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Saburo: I'll tell you. What kind of world do we live in? One barren of loyalty and feeling.
clean: I'll tell you. What kind of world do we live in? One barren of loyalty and feeling.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: I'm aware of that.
clean: I'm aware of that.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Saburo: So you should be! You spilled an ocean of blood. You showed no mercy, no pity. We too are children of this age... weaned on strife and chaos. We are your sons, yet you count on our fidelity. In my eyes, that makes you a fool. A senile old fool!
clean: So you should be! You spilled an ocean of blood. You showed no mercy, no pity. We too are children of this age... weaned on strife and chaos. We are your sons, yet you count on our fidelity. In my eyes, that makes you a fool. A senile old fool!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: [Hidetora has just signed a pledge abdicating his authority to Taro] Are you a son?
clean: Are you a son?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Taro: What do you mean?
clean: What do you mean?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hidetora: Is this a son's attitude? The hen pecks the cock and makes him a crow.
clean: Is this a son's attitude? The hen pecks the cock and makes him a crow.
